skill.name: toxicity-structure-alert
skill.description: Analyze drug molecules for toxic structural alerts using SMILES-based substructure matching with RDKit.
skill.summary: Scan molecular structures for known toxic substructure patterns and assess toxicity risk levels.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 100 static analysis findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate computational chemistry tool for drug toxicity screening. Backtick patterns in markdown files are documentation formatting, not shell execution. SMILES/SMARTS chemical patterns were misidentified as cryptographic algorithms and C2 keywords. Security checklist references to path traversal are documenting the skill's own security measures, not actual traversal. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or obfuscation was found.

content.user_title: Scan drug molecules for toxic structural alerts
content.value_statement: Drug toxicity is a leading cause of candidate failure in pharmaceutical development. This skill screens molecular structures against 20 known toxic substructure patterns to identify safety risks early.
actual_capabilities: Scans molecular structures using SMILES notation for toxic substructure patterns
Identifies 20 known toxic structural alerts including mutagenic, carcinogenic, and hepatotoxic groups
Calculates risk scores and assigns risk levels from HIGH to NONE
Generates detailed scan reports in text or JSON format
Provides specific recommendations based on detected toxicity types
Operates in degraded mode with string matching when RDKit is not available
limitations: Cannot detect unknown or novel toxic patterns not already in the predefined alert list
Results are screening-level only and cannot replace comprehensive toxicological assessment
False positives and false negatives are possible with chemical pattern matching
Requires RDKit library for full substructure matching accuracy

use_cases.title: Preclinical toxicity screening
use_cases.description: Medicinal chemists screen new drug candidates for toxic structural alerts before committing to synthesis.

use_cases.title: Regulatory safety assessment
use_cases.description: Toxicologists perform preliminary safety assessments on drug candidates during regulatory review.

use_cases.title: Lead optimization guidance
use_cases.description: Researchers evaluate structural modifications to identify safer analogs during drug discovery.

prompt_templates.title: Basic SMILES scan
prompt_templates.prompt: Scan the SMILES string O=[N+]([O-])c1ccccc1 for toxic structural alerts using the toxicity-structure-alert skill with default settings.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick screening of a single molecule

prompt_templates.title: Detailed JSON report
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the toxicity-structure-alert skill to scan SMILES O=C1OC1c1ccccc1 and return the results in JSON format.

prompt_templates.title: Full detail scan with recommendations
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a full detail scan on SMILES c1ccc2c(c1)ccc1c3ccccc3ccc21 using the toxicity-structure-alert skill. Include all alert information and recommendations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive toxicity assessment for regulatory documentation

prompt_templates.title: Python API integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ToxicityAlertScanner from the toxicity-structure-alert skill to programmatically scan multiple SMILES strings and compare their risk scores.

output_examples.output: Risk level: HIGH (score 0.50/1.0). Detected 1 alert: Aromatic Nitro (mutagenic type). May cause DNA damage and mutagenicity. Recommendation: Conduct Ames test validation and consider structural optimization.

output_examples.input: Scan C1CCCCC1 for toxic alerts in JSON format
output_examples.output: No toxic structural alerts detected. Risk level: NONE (score 0.00/1.0). Recommendation: No significant toxicity warning structures were detected, but standard safety assessment is still recommended.

best_practices: Validate that input SMILES strings represent correct molecular structures before scanning
Review both the overall risk score and individual alert details for a complete safety picture
Combine results with other ADMET prediction tools for a comprehensive assessment
anti_patterns: Relying solely on this tool for final safety decisions without experimental validation
Using the tool outside its defined scope of small molecule drug toxicity screening
Ignoring LOW risk alerts as they may indicate cumulative reactivity concerns

faq.question: What SMILES formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill accepts standard SMILES notation for small organic molecules. It uses RDKit for parsing and will reject invalid SMILES strings.

faq.question: How are risk scores calculated?
faq.answer: Risk scores are calculated from alert weights and match counts, normalized to a 0.0 to 1.0 scale. The final risk level is determined by the highest severity alert detected.

faq.question: Can this replace animal testing?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides screening-level predictions only. It cannot replace comprehensive in vivo toxicological studies required for regulatory approval.

faq.question: What is the difference between basic, standard, and full detail levels?
faq.answer: Basic shows alert names and risk levels. Standard adds SMARTS patterns. Full includes the complete description and match counts for each detected alert.

faq.question: Does the skill work without RDKit installed?
faq.answer: Yes, but in degraded mode. Without RDKit, the skill falls back to basic string matching which is less accurate than full substructure matching.

faq.question: How often are the toxic alert patterns updated?
faq.answer: The alert patterns are predefined from published literature. Updates require manual addition of new SMARTS patterns to the skill source code.
